title: Linking If and Will
learningGoal: Master the linked pronunciation of 'If I' and 'If it', and practice the contracted forms of 'will' (I'll, you'll).
instructions: If と代名詞が続く時の「音の繋がり（リンキング）」と、will の短縮形の発音を練習します。マイクに向かってリピートしてください。(Practice the linked sounds of 'if' and the contractions of 'will'. Repeat into the microphone.)

targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: The 'f' links smoothly to the 'I'. If-eye.
targetPhrasesOrWords.commonMispronunciation: Saying 'ifu ai' with a pause in the middle.

targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: The 'f' links to the 'i' in it. If-it.
The 'f' sound slides right into the 'y' sound. If-yoo.
Sounds exactly like 'aisle' or 'isle'. It's one smooth sound.
targetPhrasesOrWords.commonMispronunciation: Saying 'ai-ru' with a strong R sound.

targetPhrasesOrWords.baseTranslation: あなたは〜するだろう（you will）
彼女は〜するだろう（she will）
それは〜するだろう（it will）
targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: This is tricky. It sounds like 'id-ul' in fast speech.

targetPhrasesOrWords.baseTranslation: 私たちは〜するだろう（we will）
彼らは〜するだろう（they will）
targetPhrasesOrWords.phoneticHint: Make sure your tongue is between your teeth for the 'th'. Vay-ul.

expectedPronunciationText: If I If it If you I'll you'll he'll she'll it'll we'll they'll

feedbackRules.onPass: Great job! You linked 'if' very smoothly and pronounced the 'll' contractions well.
feedbackRules.onFail: Please try again. Don't add 'u' after 'If'. Make it one sound: 'if-eye'. Also, 'I'll' is one syllable.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good effort! Remember that 'it'll' sounds a bit like 'id-ul'. Keep practicing the 'L' sound at the end of the contractions.

adminCorrectAnswerSet.exactCorrectTranscript: If I If it If you I'll you'll he'll she'll it'll we'll they'll
acceptedVariants: if I if it if you I will you will he will she will it will we will they will
adminCorrectAnswerSet.nativeScriptReference: If I If it If you I'll you'll he'll she'll it'll we'll they'll
adminCorrectAnswerSet.transliteration: ifeye ifit ifyoo aisle yool heel sheel idul wheel vayul

audioAssets.audioTranscript: If I, If it, If you, I'll, you'll, he'll, she'll, it'll, we'll, they'll

hintText: Link the 'f' in If to the next vowel to sound natural: If I = i-fai.
commonMistakes: Putting a heavy 'u' sound at the end of 'If' (ifu ai).
